title: sideritis grandiflora subsp. baetica
intro: Sideritis grandiflora subsp. baetica is a plant species belonging to the genus Sideritis, commonly known as "ironwort" due to its historical association with ironworking. This plant is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in its dried form, known as "Baetica". The medicinal properties of Sideritis grandiflora subsp. baetica include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, Sideritis grandiflora subsp. baetica can be found in teas, infusions, and capsules, often used to support respiratory health, improve digestion, and enhance overall well-being.
